# All accepted --at formats produce a valid one-shot job. We don't
# check exact fire_at values (those are timezone-sensitive); we
# verify the job lands without error and the kind/status are correct.

exec eon add --at '+30m' --name a -- echo a
exec eon show a --json
stdout '"kind": "oneshot"'
stdout '"fire_at"'

exec eon add --at '+2h' --name b -- echo b
exec eon show b --json
stdout '"kind": "oneshot"'

exec eon add --at '+45s' --name c -- echo c
exec eon show c --json
stdout '"kind": "oneshot"'

exec eon add --at '+3d' --name d -- echo d
exec eon show d --json
stdout '"kind": "oneshot"'

# AM/PM with "tomorrow".
exec eon add --at 'tomorrow 9am' --name e -- echo e
exec eon show e --json
stdout '"kind": "oneshot"'

exec eon add --at 'tomorrow 5:30pm' --name f -- echo f
exec eon show f --json
stdout '"kind": "oneshot"'

# Tomorrow with 24-hour clock.
exec eon add --at 'tomorrow 23:59' --name g -- echo g
exec eon show g --json
stdout '"kind": "oneshot"'

# RFC3339 explicit.
exec eon add --at '2099-12-31T23:59:00Z' --name h -- echo h
exec eon show h --json
stdout '"kind": "oneshot"'
